Summary: CNS neuroblastoma with FOXR2 activation (CNS NB-FOXR2) is a novel molecular entity within CNS‑PNETs, enriched for tumors with neuroblastoma or ganglioneuroblastoma histology. Genome-wide sequencing revealed recurrent, complex inter‑ and intrachromosomal rearrangements converging on FOXR2 — including fusion transcripts that retain the full FOXR2 coding sequence, tandem duplications, upstream deletions, and a mitochondrial DNA insertion creating a novel promoter — leading to markedly increased FOXR2 expression compared with other CNS tumor types and normal brain. These promoter‑relocating and fusion events suggest FOXR2 activation facilitated by promoters of active genes, which the authors implicate in instigating oncogenic activity in this entity; gain of chromosome arm 1q was also characteristic of CNS NB-FOXR2.
References: Sturm, D., Orr, B. A., Toprak, U. H., Hovestadt, V., Jones, D. T. W., Capper, D., Sill, M., Buchhalter, I., Northcott, P. A., Leis, I., Ryzhova, M., Koelsche, C., Pfaff, E., Allen, S. J., Balasubramanian, G., Worst, B. C., Pajtler, K. W., Brabetz, S., Johann, P. D., … Kool, M. (2016). New Brain Tumor Entities Emerge from Molecular Classification of CNS-PNETs. Cell, 164(5), 1060-1072. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.cell.2016.01.015
